ogTitle: Marom Creek vs Maroubra: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Marom Creek and Maroubra.
heading: Marom Creek vs Maroubra — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Marom Creek, New South Wales and Maroubra, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Maroubra has a lower overall climate risk score (42/100) compared to Marom Creek (44/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Marom Creek or Maroubra safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Marom Creek has a flood risk score of 59/100, while Maroubra scores 49/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Marom Creek scores 57/100 for bushfire risk. Maroubra scores 36/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Marom Creek or Maroubra?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Maroubra has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.1% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
